etymology.description: 囟(囟) (xìn, "fontanel") phonetic + 心(心) (xīn, "heart") semantic -> 囟 corrupted to 田(田) early -> 思
etymology.interpretation: The character 思(sī, "to think") originally combines 囟(囟) (xìn, "fontanel") as a phonetic component with 心(心) (xīn, "heart") conveying the emotional aspect. The heart represents inner thought and emotion, while the involvement of the fontanel (an opening in the skull) originally suggested a connection to the brain, hence cognitive processes. Over time, 囟 corrupted into 田(田) due to script evolution, forming the modern character.

examples.chinese: 他一直在思考这个问题(他一直在思考這個問題)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yīzhí zài sīkǎo zhège wèntí.
examples.english: He is constantly thinking about this issue.

examples.chinese: 每次看到星星(每次看到星星)，我都会思念家人(我都會思念家人)。
examples.pinyin: Měicì kàndào xīngxīng, wǒ dūhuì sīniàn jiārén.
examples.english: Every time I see the stars, I think of my family.

examples.chinese: 沉思是获得新想法的好方法(沉思是獲得新想法的好方法)。
examples.pinyin: Chénsī shì huòdé xīn xiǎngfǎ de hǎo fāngfǎ.
examples.english: Meditation is a good way to gain new ideas.
